skill.name: umap-learn
skill.description: Guides UMAP dimensionality reduction with practical parameter tuning, visualization workflows, clustering preparation, supervised UMAP, and parametric UMAP examples.
skill.summary: Apply umap-learn for nonlinear embeddings, visual exploration, clustering preparation, and supervised dimensionality reduction.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and network indicators. Manual review found Markdown code fences, inline API signatures, and benign UMAP terminology rather than executable malware. The only residual concern is low-risk installation guidance and a promotional note for an external K-Dense web service.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are Markdown fences, inline code spans, and Python API examples. They document UMAP usage and do not execute commands when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review confirms these are Markdown documentation blocks or inline signatures. No executable Ruby, shell wrapper, or dynamic command invocation is present in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Terms Are Benign UMAP Documentation
low_findings.description: The weak crypto, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance alerts are terminology false positives. The cited lines describe UMAP speed, class separation, and embedding-space reconstruction examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language documentation and NumPy grid creation. They do not reference cryptographic APIs, host enumeration, network scanning, or credential collection.

low_findings.title: External Platform Promotion in Skill Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not malicious code, but it can influence recommendations toward an external hosted service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and located in the skill guidance. It does not override security analysis or request secret disclosure, so the impact is marketplace content risk rather than malware.

content.value_statement: High-dimensional data is hard to inspect, cluster, and reuse in machine learning pipelines. This skill gives practical UMAP guidance for tuning, visualization, supervised embeddings, and clustering preparation.
actual_capabilities: Explains core UMAP parameters such as n_neighbors, min_dist, n_components, metric, and random_state.
Provides workflows for 2D or 3D visualization of high-dimensional datasets.
Guides clustering preparation with UMAP embeddings and HDBSCAN-style validation.
Covers supervised and semi-supervised UMAP with label-aware embeddings.
Describes transform, inverse_transform, AlignedUMAP, DensMAP, and Parametric UMAP usage.
Highlights preprocessing, scaling, reproducibility, and common troubleshooting steps.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a complete automated analysis pipeline.
It cannot guarantee that UMAP embeddings preserve all global structure or density.
Large datasets may need separate performance planning and dependency management.
Parametric UMAP guidance assumes TensorFlow and related dependencies are available.

use_cases.title: Explore High-Dimensional Data
use_cases.description: Create interpretable 2D or 3D embeddings for feature vectors, biological data, text embeddings, or model outputs.

use_cases.title: Prepare Data for Clustering
use_cases.description: Tune UMAP embeddings before density-based clustering and validate whether discovered groups are stable.

use_cases.title: Design Supervised Embeddings
use_cases.description: Use labels or partial labels to improve class separation while preserving meaningful local structure.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic UMAP Plot
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose basic umap-learn settings for a 2D visualization of my dataset. Explain preprocessing and reproducibility steps.

prompt_templates.title: Tune UMAP Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my UMAP result and suggest parameter changes for n_neighbors, min_dist, metric, and n_components based on my goal.

prompt_templates.title: Plan UMAP for Clustering
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a UMAP workflow for HDBSCAN clustering. Include scaling, embedding dimensions, validation checks, and visualization guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Advanced UMAP Modes
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare standard UMAP, supervised UMAP, DensMAP, AlignedUMAP, and Parametric UMAP for my dataset and deployment constraints.

output_examples.input: I need a UMAP visualization for 100,000 text embeddings.
output_examples.output: A recommended workflow with scaling guidance, cosine distance, reproducible settings, memory considerations, and checks for misleading clusters.

output_examples.input: My UMAP plot shows many tiny disconnected islands.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting explanation that suggests increasing n_neighbors, checking feature scaling, reviewing the metric, and comparing multiple random seeds.

output_examples.input: I want to cluster customer behavior vectors after UMAP.
output_examples.output: A clustering-focused plan using more embedding dimensions, tighter min_dist, HDBSCAN validation, and a separate 2D visualization embedding.

best_practices: Standardize numeric features before fitting UMAP unless the metric and data type justify another preprocessing choice.
Set random_state when comparing embeddings, reports, or downstream model results.
Use separate UMAP configurations for visualization and clustering when density preservation matters.
anti_patterns: Do not treat visible clusters in a 2D embedding as proof of real groups without validation.
Do not use default Euclidean distance blindly for text, sparse, binary, or domain-specific vectors.
Do not compare UMAP runs without controlling preprocessing, random seeds, and parameter settings.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps plan, tune, and explain UMAP workflows for visualization, clustering preparation, and supervised dimensionality reduction.

faq.question: Does it run umap-learn automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that you can adapt in your own Python environment.

faq.question: Which UMAP parameter should I tune first?
faq.answer: Start with n_neighbors and min_dist because they strongly affect local detail, global structure, and cluster compactness.

faq.question: Can UMAP be used before clustering?
faq.answer: Yes, but use more than two embedding dimensions and validate clusters because UMAP can create artificial separation.

faq.question: When should I use supervised UMAP?
faq.answer: Use supervised UMAP when reliable labels should guide class separation while keeping meaningful structure inside each class.

faq.question: When is Parametric UMAP useful?
faq.answer: Use Parametric UMAP when you need fast transforms for new data, reconstruction, or neural network architectures for complex inputs.
